All right. Here we go. Hey, everybody. This is Russell Brunson. Welcome back to module number three of the Funnelology Masterclass, the New Art and Science of Funnel Building. I've had so much fun so far teaching this. And module number one, we talked a lot about the secret formula, which is the whole overarching strategy of business, right? Like, who is your dream customer? Where are they congregating? How do we throw out hooks and bait to grab those people? And then instead of just having a transactional business, how do we take them on a journey of transformation where we can actually change their life? What's the result we want to get to them? Now with session number one, inside of there, we spent a lot of time talking about hook story offer, right? There's so much noise happening inside of our world all the time. How do we hook them, grab their attention, just so we can tell them a story to increase the perceived value of what is we're going to offer them, and to build a relationship with them, and then we make them an offer. And I'm not talking about a product. It's the commodity we're fighting against the homeless people. How do we create a true offer that's unique, that increases the value of what is that we're offering them as well? And that was the hook story offer session. So that was all in the morning yesterday. In session number two, we started talking about the value ladder. The results, how are we taking somebody on this journey? Where do they go? They're starting a step number one, then going to number two, and number three, and we're taking them to their Oz. The Oz is the dream thing that you're able to offer them. And how do we take them on this journey? So that was session two. It's all about the value ladder. And inside of the value ladder, I talked about how at each tier of the value ladder, there's different funnels that we use. But then I also talked about the goal is not to create 10 funnels, or four funnels, or three funnels. The goal is to pick one funnel, and one funnel only, and focus on until it makes at least $1 million. Out to the two common club with that one funnel, then you can go and you can transition to funnel number two, either going up the value ladder or down the value ladder. So that's kind of where we're leaving off today. So today, we're going to be jumping into the bottom of the value ladder. I'm going to go through some of the funnels there. Now again, I'm going to be going through over the next few sessions. So we have session three. I'm going through lead funnels. Session four, I'm going through unboxing funnel session five. Presentation funnel section six is back end phone funnels. So inside of those four presentations, we're covering 10 different types of funnels. And again, I don't want you to get overwhelmed by, I've got to create all these funnels. That's not the goal. The goal is if you look at all these 10, and when this training's over, to be like, OK, that's the one I'm an execute on first. So right now, this is just really getting your mind to understanding, here are the core types of funnels that are available to me. I've been doing this now. I'm in my 15th or 16th year doing this. And so I've been geeking out for a long, long, long, long time. I've funnel hacked thousands of people. I've literally gone through every funnel I've been able to find. I buy everyone's product to see what they look like. And what's interesting is, even though every funnel's different, there's different art, there's different sales pages and videos and things like that, the funnel structure, the frameworks are the same. I would say the majority, almost all funnels, fall into one of these 10 different frameworks that I'm going to be sharing with you. So if you can understand them and start seeing them, you're going to start seeing them online. You're like, oh, that's a lead funnel. Oh, that's a survey funnel. Oh, that's this kind. And you'll be able to start seeing them. Because it's interesting, in fact, even when I wrote the Dockhumseekers book. I would say the Dockhumseekers book is the framework. It teaches you how to create the structure. So let's say there's a page like this. And there's going to be a headline that goes here. And there's going to be a video that goes here. And there's going to be a box that's got forms here. And from there they go to page number two. And on this page, there's going to be a headline here. And then there's going to be a video here. And then there's going to be a thing right here. It looks like this. And then there's going to be an order button here. And then, from here, there is going to be an upsell video that looks like the, like that's the framework. It's the scaffolding. It's the structure, right? It's the whole dot com secrets book, and this process of funnialogy is to teach you guys the funnel, the scaffolding, because almost every single funnel is the same, right? It's one of these 10 frameworks I'm going to show you. And then what makes them different, though, is this is where you weave your art into it. And that's what the whole expert secrets book is about, is how you tell your stories and your own personality, and all those things are what you weave inside of here, right? Because my funnel main like this, Tony Robbins, for they all look the same, right? The structure, the scaffolding is the same, just like in a house. Every house, the structure looks the same, like this is a kitchen, and here's a bathroom. But then the second part is the art. This is where you come in, and you're like, oh, I want purple drapes, or I want this picture on the wall, or whatever, right? This is where it's you telling your story, and you with your headline here, and this is where you're weaving in the art inside of the structure, OK? But for you guys, I just want you to understand right now, the structures are simple, and they're not difficult. So I'm going to show you guys the structure. I'm going to show you a couple examples of every single type, and you'll be like, oh, that's the structure. That's the scaffolding, right? And so it's very easy to build a funnel, and you're like, this is the scaffolding, this is the structure I need, and I just got to weave my art, my message, my stories, my videos into that structure. In fact, if you look at any of my funnels, you'll notice really quick that they're almost identical. We don't reinvent the wheel. It's like, OK, we're doing a book funnel. This is what it looks like. Oh, we're doing a car funnel. This is what it looks like. Like, the scaffolding hasn't changed. It's just the images and the videos and the art that kind of gets weaved into there. So you'll see that as we go through. I just want to kind of stress that again, so you're not like, oh, there's so many to think about. No, there's 10. There's 10 blueprints, and you're like, I want to build that one. You grab that blueprint, and you're weaving your art into it. It's really simple when it kind of comes down to it. All right, so session number three, we're going to go into lead funnels. So if you come back here to this, this is our value ladder. Again, there's four different tiers we're going to go through in the value ladder over the next four sessions. So this first session is lead funnels, then unboxing funnels, then presentation funnels, then phone funnels. So as them is moving up the value ladder, the type of funnel we use is different in each phase. The reason why is because at the bottom of the value ladder, remember if you come back to what we talked about yesterday, with the value goes right here. There's value. You've got price goes this way. If something's down here where the price is low, so maybe it's $1 or $10 or something really expensive, the value's here, you don't have to have this huge thing to sell it. I just got to convince you, I think you're going to like this. So my funnels are really simple down here. Or it might be a two-page funnel, because maybe it's even free. In fact, all the lead funnels today I'm going to show you are free. There's no money ever transacting. So I don't need a whole huge elaborate funnel to give me your email address. I see a simpler funnel, a lead funnel, where I'm going to get a lead. But as I go up the value ladder and the price of whatever I was going to sell, right now I'm asking for money, my funnel gets a little bit more complex. There's more steps to the process because it's a more complicated sell. Now I'm trying to get you to go run and grab your credit card and come back, pull that out of you all, and buy something. So because that, my funnel is going to be a little bit different, a little bit more complex, because there's more steps to get you to actually to buy something. If I'm going to move up now, I'm moving up the value ladder where the value is going up, the price is going up. So now I'm selling something maybe it's $1,000 or $2,000 or $5,000. These funnels down here won't work. I have a different sales process, something more expensive. So I come up here, and I've got my presentation funnels, which is my TV for presentation. I'm going to make a presentation to sell something. So there's different funnels that work at this level. And at the very top, when you're going $25,000, $100,000, $1,000,000 sales, it typically is hard to sell through these other vehicles. And so you switch over here to a phone funnel. And so that's kind of what we're going to be talking about. Now inside of each of these tiers, there's different funnels you can use. And so as I explained to you, they all have different reasons, different use cases. Some are like, if you're selling a physical product, this one's going to work better. If you're selling coaching, this will work better. If you're a local business, this will work better. So it's just kind of like learning them all and seeing the structure. And then for yourself, be like, OK, no one makes the most sense for my business right now. And so that's kind of how this game is played. That's why it's so important for you to kind of understand these things. Because when you understand them, then it gets really easy to start using them. I told somebody in my inner circle one time, it was Chris from Mashra actually, she came to me. And I said, I can talk to them within like 30 seconds. I know exactly what funnel I need to build, every single time. The reason is because I know it's like, it's one of these 10. And I know based on the personality, who they are, what kind of business they're at, like, OK, you're going to be best at this type of funnel, this type of funnel. And so unfortunately, I can't talk to every single one of you guys. I did 5,000 on yesterday. I have no idea what you're on today. I can't go and talk to everyone of you guys personally. So the next best thing is I'm going to spend the next couple hours going through all these, showing you guys the things, and then and kind of give you the pros and the cons and the benefits of each one. And then you've got to kind of use your brain, be like, oh, I think this is the right one for what I'm trying to do. So that's what the goal here is to help you guys understand, these are the palette of different frameworks you can use. Pick the right one, and then you're going to weave your art, your personality, your voice into those funnels. So again, in this presentation, we're focusing 100% on lead funnels. Inside lead funnels, there's three types of funnels. One is a lead or a squeeze funnel. Number two is a survey funnel. And number three is a summit funnel. So those are the three different funnels I'm going to dive into today. Now, before I go into the specifics of those three different funnels, there's a couple core concepts I need you to understand. And these are things that it took me about a year in this business before I got it. And as soon as I got this, I was like, oh my gosh, like it just changed every. It was one of those paradigm shifts that shifted my entire mindset for forever. And so I want to give you guys some of those mindset shifts because when you understand that, you'll see why your lead funnel is essential. Why should I spend time there? I'm not actually making any money. Well, there's a reason. Let me show you what that reason is. So the first concept to understand is that the money is in the list. In any type of business, this is the big secret. The money is in the list. If you look at companies that get sold, why does the company buy another company? The first time I was online, I ever saw a company get bought. It's when eBay bought Skype. And I remember looking at eBay at the time was the biggest company in the world. There were tons of money. And Skype was blowing up. And I was thinking, I bet you eBay could have really simply taken 10 of their developers and built Skype. But instead, they spent, like, I think, $2.5 billion to buy Skype. And I was like, why would they do that? I was like, oh my gosh, they're not buying Skype because of the technology. eBay's got the best developers on planet Earth. They're buying it because they want the customer base. I started looking into it. And Skype, I believe, at the time was adding 150,000 new users a day. And so I was like, oh my gosh, eBay's not buying the technology. They can build it. They're buying the customer list. The list is the secret. Why did Zuckerberg pay a billion dollars for Instagram? He could have coded it. Facebook guys are geniuses. He bought the customer list. He's buying the people. The secret in any business, the money is in the list. When you start understanding this, you start looking at your business differently. The list are the people. It's the names. It's the names and email addresses of people who have come to you. And so when you look at business through this lens, they're understanding all the money's in the list. It'll change everything for you. Now I want to do some basic marketing math. I had a mentor who told me this when I got started. He said that when you're building your list, you should be able to average $1 per month per name on your email address list. So that means that let's say you have 1,000 people on your list, you should be able to average at least $1,000 a month. If you have 10,000 people on your list, you should be able to make 10,000 a month. 100,000 people on your list, 100 grand a month, and so on and so forth. So if you know in your business, I can't want to build a business. My goal is to make six figures a year. Well, that means you've got to focus on getting 10,000 people on your list. That's 10 grand a month, times 12 months, $120,000 a year. Boom. That should be your focus point. How many 10,000 people on my list? If I do that, I'm going to have a six figure your business. If you're like, no, no, I want a seven figure your business. It's not that hard. Look at the math. Can you get 100,000 people on my list? If I do that, it's 10 grand a month, times 12 months. That's $1.2 million a year. There's the metric. So start focusing. How do I build my list? How do I build my list? Now the honest truth about it is this number of $1 per month per name is actually really, really low. Most of you guys should be able to average a lot more than that, especially. I know a lot of you guys out here are listening to our local type businesses, where you're a dentist in Boise, Idaho, or you're running Lasik in Salt Lake City, or whatever it is. So it's like, there's no way I'm going to get 100,000 people on my list. It's not 100,000 people in my hometown. The reality is, usually local businesses will average anywhere from $10 to $50 to $100 per person per name on their list. Because they're in a local business, they can have a different relationship with them. So there's the direct correlation between how much money you make from your list and your relationship you have with that list. I found people who aren't very good at this make about $1 per month per name. People who build their relationship, get to know their list, do cool trainings like this, build value, getting more value will make a lot more money from their list. But as a low bar saying, OK, I'm going to try to average $1 per month, this is how you do it. And I saw someone actually in our Facebook group the other day post, and they said, I heard us say $1 per month. Like, where does that money come from? I have a list of 10,000 people. I'm not making $10 grand a month. It's like, it's coming from all these other things. From you talking about your funnels here, and talking about here, and doing trainings, and selling products, and selling services, and becoming an affiliate for other people's products. It's all the things you're doing with that list to create value, to help find solutions to their problems, and things like that. That's where the money is coming from, is you communicate with that list. Just have me list doesn't make you any money. Having 10,000 people on a list doesn't do anything. It's learning how to communicate and build a relationship, make offers to those people. All the stuff we talked about yesterday, making a hook, telling a story, making an offer, that's where the money comes from this list. But the secret is the list. When I first was told this, I was like, oh my gosh, this is the greatest thing in the world. I got to start building a list. In fact, I have a funny story. Some of you guys may have heard me tell the story before. But after somebody explained this to me, I was like, oh my gosh, this is the greatest thing in the world. I just need a list. And so I remember about that time, I didn't know how to get a list. I saw I was on people's list. I'm like, OK, somehow people have a list. Like, I need a list. I started googling. This is, man, probably 15 years ago. I started googling how to buy, or how to get an email list. And eventually, I found this website. I think it was like spamfreeemailaddresses.com, or some variation of that. And they sold DVDs with millions of people's email addresses. And they were spam-free email addresses. I was like, oh my gosh, this is the secret. If I get an email list of a million people, I send them an email every single day. I was doing the math. Like, let's say just 1% of people bought a $20 product. I do the math. That means I'll make X amount of money. I'm like, oh, what if that's too, what if it's less than 1%? I would 1% buy it. I'd do the math on that. And I was sitting there freaking out, because I knew how much I thought I was going to be the richest kid in the world. And so I ended up buying this DVD. Took a couple weeks for it to show up. I got it. And it was a big huge Excel file with literally over a million people's names, email addresses. I'm like, this is a secret. I remember telling my wife, I was like, do you know what this is? She's like, I have no idea. I'm like, this is a DVD with a million people's names, email addresses on it. I'm like, and she's like, OK, what does that mean? I'm like, OK, I'm going to mail a million people. I'm going to sell them this product. If 1% I do the math to show her how it all works, I'm like, god, I was freaking out how rich we're going to be. And I remember telling her that night, I was like, I'm going to queue this thing up. I'm going to send out some emails. You can quit your job in the morning. You can be done. She was supporting her jobless wrestler husband at the time. I was like, you can quit your job. She's like, OK. Like, kind of, I think, not teasing me, but like, OK. Let me know if I can quit my job. So that night, I was on the computer. I got the thing set up. I got the software set up. I got the CD. I put the list on the computer, I queued up the emails. And this was back prior to high speed internet. So the kids may not remember this, but for the adults, you guys remember dial-up modems? So we had a dial-up modem and a phone line, but it was like, you only use one or the other. So I got everything queued up. Everything was working. I crawled into my computer. I unplugged the phone line. I plugged my modem in. I climbed back out. And I remember sending it for a minute. I wrote an email, and I clicked Send. And I was just like, now what? I was just like, one email sent, two email sent, three email sent, four, five, six. And I was just like, oh my gosh, this is happening. And I was freaking out like, oh, this is awesome. I remember going to bed at night, I lay in bed, and I was like, I can't even sleep. It was like Christmas or even better. Like, what's going to happen in the morning? And again, I'm doing the math in my head. I was doing the math, like how much money do you mean? I'm like, if I did this, it means I'm going to be 20 grand. If I did this, it's 50 grand. And all these different scenarios in my head as I'm laying there all excited. And finally, eventually, I passed out. Next morning, I woke up, came back out, looked at the computer. I think it was like 6,800 emails sent. And I was like, wait, what? 6,800? Like, this is going to take way longer than I thought. All my calculations are getting thrown off. I thought I sent a million emails overnight, and I'm only 6,800. I'm like, this is going to take way longer. It's going to take doing the math like three weeks to send emails out. I'm like, oh, crap, it's going to make less money. But it's OK, because right now I'm making $0, so any money will be good. And so emails are going to send out, and my wife comes out, and she's getting ready for work. And she's like, hey, I need to call somebody. I'm like, but this is printing money for like, you don't understand this printing money. And she's like, I need to use the phone. And this is pre-sell phones. I'm like, ah, so I pause the mail, or I claw back out of the desk. I go in there, and I unhook the modem, and I plug back in the phone line. And while I'm still underneath the desk, I hear the phone ring. I'm like, oh, that's weird. So I come back out, and the phone's ringing, and I pick it up, and there's some guy on the line screaming at me and cursing at me. And I'm like, what is, I don't know what's happening. And finally, after a few seconds, I realized this is my internet service provider. It was a local company doing my internet service provider. And he's yelling at me about how many spam complaints. Like, last four hours long, we've gotten like 60 different spam complaints. And I was like, no, no, no, you don't understand. Like, there's no way I'm getting spam complaints. I bought spam for email address. So in fact, you go spamforemailadjusters.com. That's how I buy these emails. People want you to send emails to them. That's how they got on the CD. It was DVD. And I still remember his, he kind of paused for a second. And he said, that's the definition of spam. And I was like, really? He's like, oh, you can't do that. I'm like, oh. And then he shut down my internet access, and everything fell apart. And I remember hanging up the phone, and I was like, oh, no. I'm dead. And then Clutz like, who is that? I'm like, oh, no one. But don't quit your job yet. Like, go to work today, and just have a good time. I'm still figuring out all the things. And I remember walking to school that day, and just being totally bummed out. My dreams and visions of retirement were here. And now I have to go back to college and pay attention. So I'm walking to school. I get to my class. I'm sitting there, and listen to teacher talk. And eventually, I snuck out, and went to the computer lab. And I checked my email. And what's crazy is that I checked my email. I looked in there, and there were a bunch of order notifications. I was like, what? I was like, oh my gosh. I logged in my PayPal account. I logged in my PayPal account. I still remember, every time I logged in my PayPal account, prior to that day, I always had balance, $0. And I opened at that time, said balance $70. And I was like, oh my gosh. Like, what I did was apparently wrong, and unethical, and even illegal. But it worked. I was like, OK. I got a little taste of success. That was amazing. I got to figure out, because there are people doing this the right way. There's got to be a right way to do it. Like, how do I build a list? And this started me on this journey of learning how to build a list. I met people and mentors and all sorts of stuff. And from that time forward, that became my focal point, is build a list, build a list, build a list. One of my very first mentors is a guy named Mark Joyner, who's still a huge mentor to this day. And he was the first one that just pounded my mind. No matter what happens, focus on building a list, building a list. Like, that is the secret of business. And it's interesting, because during that time, for those who, for my OGs out there, this is the same time that Google AdSense became Google AdSense. And during that time, what would happen is you could throw up a garbage website, throw in some ads on there, and you make six figures a month. Like, I had friends making so much money doing these things. And I'm like, oh my gosh, that is amazing. You do nothing, and you make tons of money. And Mark was like, no, that's a flash in the pan. It's going to disappear. Folks, I'm building a list. But they're making tons of money doing nothing. He's like, I know, that's going to go away. Folks, I'm building a list. I'm like, I'm like, the teenage kids are angry at their dad. Like, fine, I'm going to build my list. Like, over here, I start building my list. And sure enough, I watch a whole group of friends make insane amounts of money. And then overnight, Google slapped, and AdSense program disappeared the way that it was. There's still AdSense nowadays, but it's not like it was. For those who remember. And most of those guys never recovered, never came back into business. And I was over here on the side building my list. And that list is why I have a business today. I've been in business now 15 years, all focusing on the same thing of building a list, and building a list, and building a list. I've had times where things are great. I've had times where I should have gone through bankruptcy, because of stupid decisions I made, because of market shifts, all sorts of stuff. We're literally like, I'm meeting with a bankruptcy lawyer on the way home from the bankruptcy lawyer. I'm like, I'm going to send an email to my list. I created an offer. They hooked, tell a story, write an offer, send an email out, and boom, that day comes in $250,000 in sales. I'm like, huh, I don't have to go bankrupt. The list just funded it for me. So the list has saved me more times than anything, but it is the asset. It is the most valuable and valuable, important part of your entire business. So if you had nothing else out of this entire funnilology session, understand the value of your businesses in the list. There are people that could probably clone click funnels. Why are we so valuable as a company? We have 100,000 customers using our platform. That's the value. We've got, I don't know, 2 and 1 half, 3 million people on our email list. We've got all the social file. The lists are the value in our company. Do you guys understand that? More you understand, the better you start saying, oh my gosh, I should be focusing on building a list. I should be using lead funnels. This is not something where I'm throwing money away, something where I'm building an asset that'll pay me for the rest of my life. I was thinking about this in relation to real estate. Somebody will go and they'll spend $500,000 buying a home, cleaning it up, flipping it, getting it already, getting renters, and they're getting property manager companies and they spend $500,000 in investment to get a rent, hopefully this house cash flows them your two or three hundred dollars a month, or $5,000 a month, right? I'm like, God, you was $500,000 for $1,000 a month. What if you came back and look at our average person, like what if you spent on Facebook ads, right? Let's say Facebook ads are $3 to get a lead. $3 to get a lead, you have 1,000 people on your list, now you're making three grand a month. Like that's way easier than spending $500,000 in house and hopefully cash flowing $1,000 a month, right? Like it's just like the best investment opportunity of our generation, of our lifetime, is focusing on building your own customer list, okay? So there's the rant, I hope you got it, it makes sense for you guys. So I started going on this journey learning about list building. It was in the early days before how we build this nowadays, back when people were using these things. You remember these? You go to a website and you see a popup, a popup like, congratulations, you just won something amazing, give me your email address and I'll tell you how to claim it. And it was really annoying. And popups would come up and then, but they worked really good. Marketers started putting popups on things and they started building lists. And they're like, oh my gosh, this list building is so easy, I just put popups on places. And then companies started selling popups. You're like, I want to popup a show up on 100,000 websites or a million websites. I still remember to this day buying popup impressions. Some of you guys don't remember these days, but it was amazing. You're like, I want to buy popups on these types of websites, you write them a check and all of a sudden your popup's popping up all over the place and people are opting in. It was the greatest thing in the world. It was so easy to build a list. So I build my list this way, it's really, really fun. And then the people selling popup ads were like, wait a minute, if I can sell one popup, what if I sell two? It's all of a sudden like you got a page and there are like two people seeing this popup and they're like, oh, that worked. I got made twice as much money. And if I can sell two, what if I sell three, what if I sell three, I can sell four. Eventually you go to site, you guys remember this? Like 10 things would pop up and you're like, this is insanity, I just want to see the thing behind all these people yelling at me. And so Internet Explorer at the time got annoyed and they created popup blockers. Boom, killed all the popups. And for a season there, everyone was like, the internet's dead, we missed the burst, like we can't build lists anymore, it's impossible. And then there were some really smart dudes and I don't know who, I've heard four or five people claim that they invented it, I don't know who, but grateful that they did. They said, what if instead of coming to a website and showing a popup, what if we took the popup and that became the thing that we drove everybody to? We're like, what? Like, yeah, like literally we take the popup and then that's what the page is. And all the ads drive the popup, someone gives us an email, I just, then they get taken to the page, they want to go see. And we're like, there's no way that would work. Like let's just test it. People started doing this and shifting around this flow and oh my gosh, it worked. And this was back when they started calling these these gateway pages or squeeze pages or landing pages. There's different names for it. Basically a page that generates a lead and then takes you somewhere. And for the next decade of my life, after I've learned about these, we started doing them like crazy. So this is what a squeeze page looks like. You go and you buy ads, you drive traffic, you send them to a page, it says, hey, get your free blah. Give me your email address, I'm gonna give you something. There's an offer, give me your email address, I'll give you this, I'll give you information, I'm gonna give you a summit, I'm gonna give you a report, an e-book, a video, whatever it is, and you trade. You're making an offer, I'll give you email address for this. They come through and then people come to your website. Now what's interesting is that the big fear of people at issues is like, well if I do this, let's say I get 30% of people give me that email address, I mean 70% of people never saw the actual webpage. They said yeah, well those 30 people did come through, now you have the ability to follow up with them. You can send them email, email one, email two, and keep bringing them back. Now they're on your list and that person makes you money for forever. And after doing a million tests, for the most part, this is almost always one. The cool thing too today is also because of retargeting and other things like that, even like the 70 people who don't opt in, then you can follow them around with ads and keep pulling them back and retargeting has made this process even more powerful. So that's basically the concept. So that's list building, that's squeeze page, like some of the core fundamentals you guys understand before we jump into these three funnels. All right, so here are the three funnels I'm gonna talk about. The lead funnel, the survey funnel, and the summit funnel. The goal of all three of these types of funnels are to generate leads. For the most part, you don't make any money in these funnels, you just generate leads. In fact, a lot of times you lose money initially. That's okay, I'm gonna show you how we take someone who we lose money here and then we send them up to the next level and we actually make money here. I'll kinda show you guys how it fits in these funnels. But that's the goal of lead funnels. Not so much to make money, but to generate leads to build a list. And that's the secret of internet marketing as you now know. Okay, so let's jump into these. So funnel number one is called our lead or our squeeze funnel. People call it squeeze, they call it squeeze because someone's coming through this page and you're squeezing their email address from them. So people call it lead squeeze or squeeze funnel or just lead funnel. There's a lot of different names in the industry. That's kinda what it is. All right, so a lead funnel is very simple. There's basically two pages. Page number one, somebody comes and you're gonna give them what we call a lead magnet. The lead magnet is the offer. If you give me your email address, I'll give you this thing. And the sexier your offer is, the more likely someone's gonna go and give it to you. It draws the lead magnitude and they're like, oh, I want that thing, cool, I'll give it to you. Just give me your email address. You guys have seen this a million times. It's not something new to you. But you're on Facebook and you're like, hey, get it for your report, you click on it, it takes you a page, like give me your email address, I'll give you the report. Here's the lead magnet, that lead magnet got you from Facebook, got you to my landing page and now it's gonna give me your email address. That's what we call the lead magnet. I'll talk about those here in a little bit. The first page is the giveaway lead magnet and the second page, you give them the lead magnet. Thank you for giving me your email address. Here's the thing you just asked for. It's very simple, two pages. You can build these in like 30 seconds. In fact, I'm gonna show you guys our number one squeeze page, our number one lead funnel right now. It's generated over a quarter million leads in the last year. I built it in less than 10 minutes and you're like, that's not possible Russell, I'm gonna show it to you and you're like, oh my gosh, that is the ugliest, simplest thing ever. Quarter million leads, give myself a quarter million dollar a month raise, just from this one lead funnel. Super simple. Okay, the first squeeze page I ever saw was from a guy named Evan Pagan. His pen name at the time was David D'Angelo and he owned a company called Double Year Dating. And this was the very first one I had ever personally seen. Okay, I remember seeing this and I was like, this is the weirdest thing I've ever seen. And I read it and said, you're about to learn the secrets that most men will never know about women. Inside, you're gonna learn the kiss test. How to tell if she's ready to be kissed. They're gonna see men and women and think about dating and has big, bold lists and stuff, you're gonna learn. So that's the lead magnet. Give me your email address, I'm gonna teach you the kiss test. Okay, I remember seeing that and I was a bunch of marketing seminars back a decade ago, people were talking like, do you see Evan Pagan's doing? Do you see his lead page? And like, we'd go to his squeeze page and we'd go to there and I was like, this is so crazy. And remember looking at it, I was like, I wanna know what's on the other side of that page. But I had just gotten married to my beautiful wife, Collette. And I was like, if I put my name and email address in here, I'm gonna get emails about how to pick up ladies. And my wife's gonna see these and she's gonna be like, what are you researching online? So I remember like, I came in probably a dozen times. I was like, I need to opt in but I'm scared to put my email address in. And so I remember asking, actually showing her my K-Collette. This is a marketing experiment. This is a guy, I don't, like I love you. I don't wanna date anyone else ever but I need to see what's behind this page. And she's like, you okay if I put my email address in? And she's like, yeah, that's weird. I'm like, okay, cool. So I put my email address in and what's crazy is I didn't, again, I didn't know what's gonna be on the next page. I thought maybe it's like a 25 page reporter, a big PDF or a video series or something. And what was on the other page was so simple. On the second page, it took you to this little paragraph here that explained the kiss test. This was the lead magnet, okay? And basically what it says, it said, when you go to the doorstep of the girl, you walk up the doorstep and you talk to her really closely and you go and you try to put her, like if her hair comes down or something, you go through your hand and try to put your hair either behind her hair or behind her ear or behind her, whatever, right? So you reach up and you do that. And so what's gonna happen is one of two things, okay? If the girl kind of pulls back a little bit, it means, like a board, do not kiss her, she does not wanna be kissed, okay? If she goes like this and she turns into you, that means she's ready to be kissed. You go from there, boom, and apparently that's how you do it. I don't know, that was before my time. I was already married at this point so I haven't had a chance to test it. But apparently that's how it works, okay? So that was it, it was a paragraph where he explains the kiss test. That was it, okay? At the time, I remember back a decade ago, this company was doing $20 million a year and that was where they drove 100% of traffic was to this page right here. And the next page said, here's the kiss test and then if you read the rest of the paragraph, it's like, if you think that's good, we have a whole e-book called W-8. Click on the link down below to buy the e-book. That was it, that was the business model, okay? So simple, it's so powerful, okay? So let me show you how it works. This is a very simple lead funnel from Brian Rand. Brian Rand's got like the most simple, clean design and I love his stuff, anyway. So this is an example, right? He created this little PDF called the Going Pro Manifesto, step number one, he creates the lead magnet, step number two, he trades it for the email address, okay? Very simple, so there's page number one, then a couple of things on this, again, simplicity, the more simple your landing pages are, the better, okay? If you try to get people a million bells and whistles and options and opportunities and flashing gifts and links and everything, it's gonna destroy everything, right? You know, there's no logo, there's no menu bar, there's no nothing, right? It's, here's the offer, here's what it looks like, give me an email address, that's it, okay, it's simple. It gives curiosity, okay? Every time I have a landing page that like, the conversions are low, the more I just increase the curiosity, the better, okay? So the headline, like, they're right now, download for free now, change of business forever, like, that's not super curiosity-based, I might change it to something like, download the Going Pro Manifesto to find out how to go from blank to blank without doing blank, right? Something like that, and also that cranks up the curiosity which will increase conversions, more people will want that thing, because they're like, ah, what is it I need to know, okay? And the third step is it tells them what to do, okay? It says download the thing, enter email address down below, okay? It's not just leaving it to their own imagination, it tells them specifically, enter your email address down below to discover the proven principles I used to, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? Tell them, go do this, put your email address in, don't assume people will know to do that, as much as you would assume people would know to do that, tell them what to do, okay? Okay, and then page number two is basically you give them the lead magnate's ask for, okay? We'll talk about product launch funnels tomorrow, it's up here, they go from lead funnel right here, boom, directly into their product launch funnel, okay? We see how simple and easy it is, very cool, and this is one of the things I wanna add, this is called the reverse squeeze page, I'm showing this as an example, because a reverse squeeze page is basically, typically with most squeeze pages, we wanna make them as simple and curiosity as the basis possible, it gives us ability to get the most leads possible, some businesses, you don't want insane amounts of leads, right, like let's say you're a local dentist, you probably don't want 5,000 leads a day coming into your funnel, right? You wanna be able to qualify leads better, so reverse squeeze page gives you the ability to qualify leads, you will get less leads as people come through, but the quality leads will get higher, so what happens here is, they come here, they see the headline, they watch the video, on the video, I spend three to five minutes explaining what the lead magnet is, okay, and then from there, I get people to put the email address in down below, and again, that barrier, then watching a two minute video will dramatically decrease how many leads you get, but the quality ones who come through the other end will dramatically increase, okay, so if your business is like, I want less leads, or else I want higher quality leads, then I recommend doing reverse squeeze pages, basically you're pitching them up front, you're spending time on video explaining what the thing is, first, which again will lower the number of leads, but dramatically increase the quality of the leads that come through, so that's what reverse squeeze page is, if you ever hear me talk about it or somebody else, it's just simply a squeeze page that you're selling up front, and explaining more what it is, so it has a barrier in getting people to come in. Okay, so now some of you guys are like, this is amazing, Russell, but like, I'm not a creative lead magnet, how do I do it? Well, I wanna show you how simple it can be. This is Brendan Bruchard's one page productivity planner, this is one of his friend and lead funnels, and it's simple, it's literally one page, okay, it's a piece of paper, one piece of paper, okay? If you want his one page productivity planner, you go here, you put your email address in, and then the next page, he gives you the productivity planner, and then he sends you to the next funnel, his value ladder, okay? So I want you to think of it, it can be as simple as that, it doesn't have to be like my black book I created is this huge long project, but I am intense, I've got a staff of almost 500 or almost 400 people here, I'm gonna do crazy stuff, for a lot of you guys, you don't have the staff or the team or the manpower, it doesn't matter, you don't need that, it doesn't have to be complex, it has to be something that's sexy, it's exciting. One page productivity planner used by CEOs and achievers worldwide is sexy, right? 70 headlines is sexy, you know, 500 top keywords are sexy, you're looking for something simple and easy that someone's gonna be excited for, okay? Yesterday, during module number one, we talked about creating offers, right? We talked about having a potential, I mean, if I'm born of all the potential products you could possibly create, okay? Well, I do that, I start looking at that board and like, here's all the potential products, which one of these things is like the sexiest, like the most exciting, I could take that thing that's already been created or might create and make that the thing I give away on the landing page, okay? So that's kind of what I'm looking for when I do stuff like that, okay? So start thinking about like, what do you have that you can give away as a lead magnet, okay, could you refer to a report, some white papers, a free video? A lot of times, the thing I'm giving away is the video they're watching on the next page, which might be the sales video from right here, okay? Maybe it's a free workshop, which could be, you know, the next piece or whatever it might be. Coupons, okay, local business, I was like, well, how do I use these lead funnels, Russell? Well, give away a coupon, give away a 10% discount, give away a special night, you know, for people in your local community, give away a lunch seminar, like a whole bunch of things you can do there. Okay, coupons work great for local businesses, service-based businesses. Anyway, you're looking for access for something awesome, okay, information products are usually the simplest way. In fact, for most companies, no matter what you're doing, I would still create some kind of information product because they're simple to make, they're free to deliver, and the perceived value of them is very, very high. Software, if you can create a simple software program, another way to make an amazing, insane lead magnet as well. Okay, so that is the funnel number one, which is the lead or squeeze funnel. Tim, you guys ready to move on to funnel number two? Okay, funnel number two is very powerful, it's a little bit more complicated, but there's a time and a purpose for this as well. If you're just getting started, I wouldn't start with this funnel, but this is one, as you're growing, can do amazing things for you. It's called the survey funnel. So this is how a survey funnel works. In fact, yeah, I'll just show you on the slides. I wrote survey funnel three pages, actually it has more than three pages, I messed up there, so this is the survey funnel. So on the first page of survey funnel, you're gonna have a survey, then from there, the goal of the survey is basically this. I'm gonna write this down. The reason why survey funnels work great is that the product that we're selling has multiple different markets that may want it, right? So let's say I'm selling whatever, say I have a course, right? Let's say the course I'm selling is for, it could be for, say it's for married people, right? So maybe it's for married people, but it also could be really good for people who are dating, or maybe it's also really good for parents who are married or something, or no kids, and this one's great for with kids or whatever, right? So there's different segments that could want your product, right? Let's say I'm trying to sell you the product, I'm pitching you, and I have a whole sales video in training where I explain to you why this is such a great thing to do as you're dating, you're like, well, I'm already married, I don't need to buy this product. You're like, oh, well, it works for you too if you are dating, but you've been pitching me on all the benefits of this, and you said it's really great for you to know kids, but I actually have kids, like, well, it works for everyone. Also, you're like, it's hard to sell through a funnel, right? And there's like different segments of people you're talking to. Okay, I had the same problem with click funnels, it's like, somebody comes into my world, and they're like, how do I use a funnel? Well, what kind of business do you have? Like, well, I'm an author, speaker, coach, okay, cool. Well, if you're an author, this is how the funnel's I would use, and somebody else is like, well, that's cool, but I'm a network marketing. Oh, well, if you're network marketing, here's the funnel you should use. And somebody else is like, well, I'm a local business. I'm like, oh, cool, if you're a local business, and so there's different segments of the market that I have. And so if I have one funnel speaking, all of them, it can be really, really difficult to be able to explain to all of them how this works for them specifically. Does that make sense? So that's where SurveyFundal comes in place. Where basically you have this page right here in the middle, where you ask some questions. You say, okay, let me ask you some questions. You know, what kind of business do you have? How many employees do you have? What do you do? And you ask them a survey with a bunch of different questions, and from that survey, you're able to figure out, are there this kind of person, this kind of person, this kind of person? Okay, so the survey goes through, and by the time it's done, you figure out, okay, this person's a network marketer. So then you redirect him directly to this page. We're like, oh, this person's an infant, you know, as a coach, I'm gonna redirect him to this page. Oh, this person's, it's something else, you redirect him to this page. Because that's basically the process of a survey funnel. It's to figure out, of all the traffic you're driving, who is every single person, and then figure out, okay, this is who they are, now go take them to a page that specifically sells to that type of person, okay, and that's the value in this. So like I said, to begin with, it takes a lot of time to put some of this together, but after you have it, it's amazing, because now you can speak specifically to each person, each customer segment you have, in a way that sells specifically to them, okay? All right, so let me just kind of walk you through how this funnel, how this funnel works. So this is the old ClickFunnels homepage. We ran this for over a year, and it's still, the survey's still on the homepage, it's just down a little bit lower. But for a year, this is our page, because people always ask me, what kind of funnel works for my business? So we have the whole top block here, be like, okay, ClickFunnels gives you everything you need, let us know what kind of company you have, and we'll be able to tell you which funnel is best for your specific business, okay? And we figured out, these are the different segments we had, we had agencies, information product people, e-commerce, coaching, consulting, network marketing, local businesses, so we had all these different people here, right? So somebody would come to that, and they fill out the first step of the survey, and then there's a second question, third question, fourth question, fifth question, we ask a bunch of questions. Okay, now when you're doing a survey like this, typically there's one question, and Ryan Levesque wrote a book called Ask, that kind of teaches this methodology, it's really good. And in his book he talks about, every time you survey like this, there's one question that's the bucket question. The bucket question is the question to let you know which bucket to put somebody into, okay? And so while I'm asking a bunch of questions, the only one I really care about is my bucket question. The bucket question identifies who they are. The other question is about getting them to engage, and you can think about it, about themselves, and their business, and then one question, someone on that survey is your bucket question. Okay, so you can build these really simply inside of ClickFunnels, obviously, and in there you identify, this is my bucket question, and based on the bucket question, where do we redirect them to, okay? So they come in here, they answer a bunch of questions, after we've figured it out, then we have a page right here, and this is the little flashing gift, it's like we are searching for your profile, lulululu lululu lululu, and then it pops out, oh, based on who you are, here you go, here, if you're a network marketer, like this one will pop up. If you say that you're a non-profit, this one's gonna show up, if you're selling information product, this one's gonna show up, okay? And so we've got 10 different variations of this, but based on who the survey tells us you are, you're gonna land on these pages, you then put your email address in, and you put on a specific email list for that segment. Now you can send followed emails specifically for network marketers, specifically for local businesses, specifically for non-profits, right? Think of the email sequences, and then after they put email address in there, redirects them to a sales page, that's specifically for them, okay? So if you're a network marketer, you're gonna get an email sequence about network marketing, and you're gonna land on this page right here, in Mindy and Manny, talking about how they use funnels for the network marketing business, okay? If you are a local business, you're gonna pop out, you're gonna see Yanni's video right here, and this is Yanni, who's a painter, talking about how he uses funnels for his painting business, okay? If you're selling information products, boom, you see Natalie's video talking about how Natalie uses information products, funnel information products, right? And we did 10 different variations of this. Okay, so that's kind of how it works. So if you walk through, here's the survey, finally come here, then they go through the survey, they answer a bunch of questions, we figure out which bucket they fall into, and then based on the bucket, they see one of these landing pages, one of these squeeze pages, they put the email address in, and then boom, they're taken directly to the sales page that speaks specifically to who that person is. That's the magic of a quiz funnel. You guys see how this works? Okay, these are, again, they're simple, but man, they're simple in thought. It takes a while, because for us, we had to write 10 different sales letters, but if you got 10 different email sequences, like it takes a long time, in fact, inside of our company, when we started this project, we called it Project Mother Funnel, because it was such a big project, and so we created the whole thing out, but that's kind of what it is and what it became. So that's how a survey funnel works. So if you start thinking about, as my company's growing, I have more time and energy and resources to be able to create something like this, like what are the different customer segments I need to speak to, right? And then from there, you can create special sales messages specifically for each one. They opt in here, if you come back to this page right here, right? The lead funnel's down here, they opt in, and then for us, as soon as they identify which one they're in, then boom, they're moved into a presentation funnel, when I always sell them on the benefits of click funnels. All right, so that's how a survey funnel works. Here's another one, this is one of my favorite ones, this is an acidity quiz. So how acidic is your body? Take the test, you click on the button there, and then boom, I ask you a bunch of questions to figure out your age, your weight, how you're eating, and somewhere in there, there's a bucket question, they identify where you fall in, and then boom, it pops out, I'm generating results from there. Cool, we figured out exactly the reports for you, put your email address in here, you put your email address in, and boom, it pops out the entire report for your specific acidity level. I take in this quiz like 5,000 times, and every variation has a different cells letter, different thing, different email sequence, and you get redirected based on how you fill out the survey. So that's what a survey funnel is. And hopefully you guys are liking this and geeking out. So we gotta see, how many of you guys want to do a survey funnel now? It's a fun, big, exciting project. All right, now funnel number three, the last of our lead funnels we're gonna talk about is a summit funnel. And a summit, and you guys have probably seen, we did two summits last year, we'll probably do two more this year, we keep doing these summits because they are amazing ways to generate leads. The summit becomes the lead magnet. And you guys wanna summit is, we're interviewing a whole bunch of speakers on a certain topic. So the summit is the actual lead magnet. Give me your email address, I'm gonna give you access to this entire summit with a whole bunch of people, who are gonna help you on whatever the topic is. So the summit becomes the lead magnet. So this is what a basic summit funnel looks like. Page number one, there's a video or something talking about here's what the summit is, register to get access to it. And down below you see like, here's all the speakers that are gonna be speaking in the summit. So that's page number one. And here's an example of our Philly Bootcamp. Well, I guess I've gone through our Philly Bootcamp, it's an example of a summit funnel. There's a video here on top, basically saying, I'm gonna teach you guys how to get in 100 days to turn your hobby as an affiliate into a full-time career, like going through the Philly Bootcamp, they watch the video, that's step number one. Now the one thing about a summit funnel is that really quickly it pushes you up here to start selling. So it's the only one of our lead funnels you start selling at immediately. Okay, so right here is there's an upsell. Now everyone does summits in different ways, typically when someone's selling a summit, their upsell right here is gonna be like an all access pass. It's like, hey, you're gonna get the summit, but it's gonna expire every day, the summit's live for a day, then you lose that lead magnet, disappears. If you pay for an all access pass for 100 bucks, whatever, then we're gonna give you guys all the interviews and they're there forever, you can listen to them, if you want, plus we're gonna give you the transcripts and this and a whole bunch of other things to increase the value of the offer, right? And so this is where a lot of people do that, is that they have the upsell right here, and the upsell will typically cover all the ad costs that you have, that you're driving ads into the summit funnel, okay? So you're getting leads for free because that upsell there covers your ad costs. Now for me, I did it a little differently because my biggest goal was to get people into the one funnel way challenge, which I'll talk about after the break when we come back talking about challenge funnels, but for me, challenge is one of the most powerful tools we have right here at the unboxing level, so I go from the lead magnet and then getting the summit immediately to here, I think I'm one of the only people I've ever seen do that. Most people go directly from here and sell all access pass or some kind of offer related to the summit, okay? All right, so that's page number two for me is again, and I kind of do the same thing, they get the all access pass for free when they join the one funnel way challenge, okay? And then down here, what happens then is that after they opt in, each day they will get a page like this, it's like, okay, here's the first five speakers, and have until you get 24 hours to go listen to it, right? So it's the lead magnet that kind of disappears. So they have 24 hours to go listen to interview one, interview two, interview three, interview four, interview five, okay? And then after 24 hours, it disappears. Then the next day comes out, okay, here's the next five interviews, they listen to those, and then those disappear after 24 hours, the next one comes out again, the life of 24 hours, they disappear. The reason I make it disappear is because the upsell gets the all access pass, so by making them disappear and taking them offline, there's more reasons why people want to go and buy the all access pass. They may miss one of the interviews, or maybe miss day two, because they're traveling, or maybe they couldn't consume it all, because it's like 40 hours of interviews, and like, I want to watch it later, then the all access pass, the value of that dramatically increases, and becomes more valuable, okay? And so throughout the entire challenge, I keep selling that all access pass, excuse me, throughout this entire summit, I keep selling the all access pass, get access to that as well, okay? The biggest thing to understand is that the summit funnel, it's a lead magnet, the summit is the lead magnet. You give me an email address, and you give access to this amazing summit, and there's a bunch of really fun, cool, unique ways to monetize it on the back end, okay? We do the same thing with our 38 challenge, same kind of concept, right? So we came in here, there's a video explaining, like hey, give me an email address, and we get access to this challenge, where I interview a whole bunch of people about it, they were to lose everything, I had no money left, what would you do over the next 30 days to get back on top, okay? That's the lead magnet, they give me the email address, and then boom, here I have to sell them on one funnily way challenge, and they get access, all access pass as well, and then boom, day one, day two, day three, they get the videos, and they get pulled away, they get the videos, and they pull away, okay? Now typically the way that most people do it, the first time you do a summit, usually it's live, live, right? So it's like, the lead magnet you're driving traffic to, it's like hey, next week or next month we're doing this summit, it's gonna be amazing, get, you know, put your email address now so you can sign up for the summit that's coming in the future, so people spend a couple months doing all the, a couple weeks building it up, getting a big list of people, and then the summit launches live, and that's how we do ours the first time, and then afterwards we set it evergreen, so you can make it evergreen for like, right now if you go to 30days.com, you can see it's funnel, it's live, if you go to philibookam.com, you can see that funnel, it's live, okay? There was a time we launched them live, but now that they're done and they're in there, evergreen, and that's kinda how the funnels work now, and both these funnels generate tons of leads for us all the time, 24 hours a day we're driving traffic into both 30days.com and philibookam.com, and they're great lead generators for us as well, okay? Here we can see the migration of nearly 5,000 funnel hackers descend upon an unsuspecting hotel. For a brief period of four days, this habitat will dramatically change. The funnel-less hacker form is so rare species, but ever growing by the thousands each passing day. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings. Rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment, and of course, recording more ads because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. You want another? You got one? Yeah! This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro-yoga, and handshakes, from the field to the bush. In the early hours, they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race towards seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there, they revel at the arrival of the alphas, who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time, their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom.
