So I want to make sure you guys don't miss that. So hang out for that as well. Yeah. With that, so we can jump into unboxing. You guys ready? Are we ready? Okay. As I'm walking up here, this is your shot. Give me your biggest aha right down below. Okay. I'll get started here like 10 seconds. All right. Here we go. Hey everybody, it's Russell Brunson. Welcome back to session number four of our new funnology masterclass, the new art and science of funnel building. Hope you guys have been enjoying all of the sessions so far. So as you know, we started out session number one, talking about secret formula and hook story offer session two. We talked about the value ladder and then session three that we just recently completed. We talked about the bottom tier, the value ladder lead funnels and how to get people into your, to join your list, how to get them to become leads and how we use the three types of lead funnels to get people and grab them and start sending them up to the next phase of the value ladder. So right now we're going to start moving up to this phase. This whole presentation is about the second phase of the value ladder. And I like to call these type of funnels unboxing funnels. And the reason why is two years ago we were working on a presentation for Funnel Hacking Live. And I was looking at all the types of funnels and just trying to figure out how do we simplify this process and this concept for people. There's a whole bunch of different things. And as I was sitting there, I had this idea, I was like, wait a minute, these are like unboxing funnels. And it gave me this huge light bulb in my head. And I'm hoping that you guys see this will give you the light bulb as well and help you understand like why we call these things unboxing funnels. Like how they fit into the whole grand scheme of everything we're doing. So we'll come back here again. Here's the value ladder. All the funnels we were talking about. So we did lead funnels earlier and right now we're going into unboxing funnels. Now inside here are the three core funnels we're talking about. One are book funnels. This is mostly for like information type businesses. Number two is cart funnels which are more for e-commerce type businesses. Number three are challenge funnels which work for all types of businesses which is cool. Okay, so this is where the concept of unboxing comes from. So on day number one we talked about hook story offer, right? I spent a lot of time talking about how one of your guys is... Let me flip this for I keep talking. One of the most important things you can be doing in your business is building up an inventory of products so you can start bundling together and make offers, right? So we talked about like having this whole thing and potential products, right? And sitting down with your team and be like, okay, what else can we offer our people? We're like, oh, we could do... What if we did this? And we go, what if we did this? And like just making this list of as many different ideas of different products that you could create to help serve your dream customer, right? They could help give them the a-ha they need or the big change or whatever the thing might be, right? You know, this sounds as many different ideas as possible. And remember I talked about when I did it that I was like, okay, we could... What if we give a book about this? What if we give them an MP3 plan? What if I interview five people about this? And what if I didn't fight in my house and do... Just listing out all the potential things we could do to help them get the result, right? So you followed session one, you should have this or should know at least how to do this crazy potential product. And then I said from there, what we're gonna do is we're gonna make an offer slide, right? So here's your offer. And from that, say, okay, if somebody buys this offer from me, I'm gonna take this component and this is gonna be the first thing they get, right? And then from here, I'm like, oh, this will be the second component and this will be the thing over here. And then here's gonna be the third component and then the fourth component. There's all these different things, right? And then this becomes the actual offer. You're gonna get the total value of this equals whatever, right? So this is the stack slide, right? We've talked a lot about stack slides, but that's where it comes from. Here's my inventory of all potential things I could create. From there, I'm gonna take some things and this becomes my offer. Now, in the lead funnel one, we talked about in the last session, literally you could go and say, okay, this one right here, this part of the offer, this could be the lead magnet. I'm gonna give away my lead funnel, right? So this could be your lead magnet, right? So that's kind of from yesterday's. From today's, we're coming through and we're saying, okay, I'm gonna build an offer. So here's my stack slide and there's two ways now. Now that we have this offer, create this two ways to sell it, okay? The one way I think most of you guys are probably more familiar with is what we're talking about during the next session, which was the presentation funnel, okay? So my image over here, the presentation funnel is basically me doing a presentation, right? A webinar, Facebook Live, me speaking on stage, things like that, where I'm taking this offer and this is where you guys probably see me do this time-time. Where I do have a whole presentation and at the end of it, I do my stack, where I said, the first thing you're gonna get is this and then you're gonna get this. I do this big huge stack slide and we sell the whole offer, right? So after we create an offer, that's one way to sell it through a big presentation, okay? And during the presentation, we'll talk about that. But the second way is we do it through an unboxing funnel. We take this offer and now I'm gonna flip it on its side, I'm gonna unbox it and take it out of the box, okay? So let me show you what that looks like here on the slides. Okay, when we launched the Expert Seekers book, I was thinking, like, if I was to sell a $1,000 version or $500 version of Expert Seekers, like, what would it look like? Like, what I could have the Expert Seekers book and the audio book and the home study course and the traffic secrets home study course, like, these are all things that I could bundle and offer, right? And if I was gonna do a webinar and sell a $1,000 version or $500 version on a webinar, I would do a presentation and this would be the first thing you're gonna get is the Expert Seekers book, then you get the audio book, right? Like, I would do an intemporary presentation. With an unboxing funnel, what I'm doing is I'm taking that offer and I'm pulling out the pieces, right? Where this first thing, the book becomes the actual offer on the landing page. Okay, the second thing, the audio book becomes the order form bump, okay? The next home study course becomes upsell one and the next home study course becomes upsell two. So I'm taking this offer that we designed and I'm pulling it out. I'm unboxing and pulling out these things and I'm putting them into logical sequential order, okay? The first thing that I'm gonna buy is the book. Then I buy the book and the next logical thing is that you should buy the audio book. The next logical thing is the next, the next, the next. Okay, so that's why I call it an unboxing funnel because you notice all these funnels, it's like taking the same offer I would sell for $1,000 and tipping it outside. Okay, a good, I did a podcast about this probably almost a year ago now. But it was at this time we were launching the 10X Secrets Masterclass, which was basically how to speak and sell from stage. And in that course, we made the first offer slide. It was like, okay, there's the three hour training I did teaching people the perfect web of our presentation. Then I did a three hour training where I went through my presentation, 10X and walked through slide by slide, what I did and how I did and showing all the intricacies of what I did. And from there, I took, I got John Childers who were my first public speaking training and then I got him to license us, his Childers Chunks whole training course that used to sell for $10,000. I got licensed from that course and then I got all my speaking mentors coming to do trainings on there. So I had Armand Moran and all these other people in there. And then I had all these things, right? And the stack was huge. It would easily have been at least $1,000 and maybe $2,000 product, okay? And we were gonna go and sell it through a webinar and all that kind of stuff. In fact, that was like the plan and then I was like, you know what? I wanna show people that you don't always have to sell through a webinar, right? Like that is one funnel type, okay? It's a great funnel type. It's what we do up here, right? This is one of the types of presentation funnels, but you don't have to that way. Instead, I said, let's take what I typically sell on the webinar and let's pull it out, okay? And if you remember going through that funnel, we launched the 10X Secret Home Study Course. It was $297. When you bought that, then there was an upsell for, okay, I remember now. I think it was $497 for the next thing, an upsell of like $297. But if you took all the price points of all the different pieces in the funnel, it added up to be the same $997 that I would have sold it for if I sold it in a huge bundle. Does that make sense? So that's kind of, that's what I'm talking about here. That's how this whole thing works. So I'm unboxing. I'm taking the offer that I created and instead of just selling it all at once, I'm unboxing it and making lower ticket price things that we go and we put into a funnel like that, okay? All right, so there's two types of core unboxing funnels. And they're very, very, very, very similar. But there's one or two big differences that are hard to see with the naked eye. So I'm going to walk through these. But the two core ones, number one is like an e-com cart funnel. We're going to go deep into that. So a cart funnel is what most e-commerce people use, okay? If you're selling any kind of physical products on Amazon or Shopify or Etsy or whatever, this is the type of funnel that you will almost always use, okay? And the other type is the book funnel. I'll talk about this in a second, but it's not just books. It's any kind of information product that fits into a book funnel. We call it a book funnel because that's what most people use it for, but it works with not just books. I'll show you here in a minute, okay? So these are the two core unboxing funnels that you'll see 99.9% of the time. I have no idea if that's true, but the majority of the time is one of those two funnels, okay? And then again, at the end, I'm going to talk about challenge funnels because it's another type of unboxing funnel that's powerful as well. Okay, so with that said, you guys ready to jump into this? All right, let's go into funnel number one, book funnels. Okay, I need to update some of these images. Book funnel has more than three pages. So this is the book funnel. It has more than three pages. I like the top there, but this is the book funnel, okay? So there's a sales page, a bump, upsell one, upsell two, and thank you page. Okay, so that's what a book funnel actually looks like. Okay, now again, I mentioned this a second ago, but a book funnel aren't just for selling books, okay? The structure works really good for selling books, but it works for any type of information product. In fact, for most of you guys, I recommend not writing a book right away because writing a book takes a long, long, long time. I just finished my third book and it's hard. So there's a point in a time when you should write a book, but prior to that, you can use a book funnel to sell stuff that's a lot faster. For example, this is I used a book funnel for this, and it wasn't me selling a book, it was me selling a preloaded MP3 player with a whole bunch of episodes from the marketing and your car podcast. I'm not going to use the marketing and your car podcast, okay? So I took the same book funnel structure, but instead of selling a book, I sold information on an MP3 player, okay? This is the same thing I did with the perfect webinar script. I took the same book funnel structure, but I didn't have time to write a book on this, so I gave it away with one piece of paper. They had my script on it with a DVD. They explained it. So it's a CD and a DVD. So this information doesn't have to be a book. Any kind of information works really good through a book funnel, okay? All right, so we'll walk you through the process. So the first step in a book funnel is the two-step order form like these, okay? You've seen this dot com secrets book. You've seen there's expert secrets book. The traffic secrets book will be similar. That's kind of the page structure. Now, I'm going to walk through, I'm going to explain to you guys how we kind of figured this out. So if you look at the two-step order form, the order form is close to the top because the offer is low price point, right? If I was selling something more expensive, like $100 or above, I would not put the order form at the top, okay? It's too fast. I put the order form down below lower, but for lower ticket things, I put the order form here. Now, I personally used to hate this look and feel. So if any of you guys are like, I hate this, I don't have an order form at the top, like I get it. I hate it as well. In fact, probably a decade ago, I started studying from these marketers. There's different tiers of marketing. There's like, oh, there's not like the good guys and the bad guys, but there's like light and dark and there's a bunch of shades of gray in the middle, right? And I've always been on the marketing, like the lighter side of things. But I met these guys who were definitely more on the darker gray to black hat businesses, but they were making insane amounts of money. So I was like, I want to learn from these guys. What do they know that I don't know? And so I got into some groups, I met these guys and started talking to them. And it was interesting because they started showing me some of their funnels. And I started looking at pages and most of their pages had similar structures. They had the order form on the right hand side and they had their name, their email address, their full shipping address, all sorts of stuff. And I was like, that's crazy. You're asking for all this stuff before they even know what you're selling. Like, why would you do that? And they're like, we've tested a billion different things. These guys were like scientists. Like, they literally would hire people at colleges close to them. They had like computer science degrees and mathematics degrees and all sorts of stuff that happened to do insane amount of split testing because they were driving so much traffic. And so they showed me these pages and I was like, I don't like those. They said, well, the way it works is step number one, we asked for the shipping address. And step number two, we asked for their credit card. They said, if we asked for the credit card, page number one, it dramatically decreases conversion because the risk is too high for the person. And we had all these mathematical reasons and all these split tests they'd done. And they were showing me. And I was like, oh, my gosh. And so I fought it for years. I was like, I'm never going to do that kind of thing. I don't like it. And in my mind, it was like, that's something that these kind of marketers use. And then one time I decided, I'm just going to test it. These have been testing that crazy thing. Could I do it in a way that's not the black hat side where we're selling good products, good things, but using the techniques and the things that we learned over here? And so we did our very first version. It was before Click Phone. It was before .com Secrets Book. It was another offer we had made. And we tested it out. And I did the thing with everything being above the fold, just like this. There's the video, the order form. And we tested it. And I tried to model theirs as close as I could. And I kid you not. Out the gate, it did five or six X higher conversions than anything we'd ever done prior to it. And I was like, oh, my gosh. Like, why did I fight that so long? And so we built Click Funnels initially. I remember Todd had the original order forms. I was like, is there any way we can build an order form that takes that whole two-step element that we love, that we use, that we have to custom code every single time and make it a thing? And so he did. And so for now, all of you guys, if you want to do a two-step order form, you click a button and boom, it pops the thing in there. It is one of the most amazing things on Earth. So that's kind of some background story of such a geek on this stuff. Like, I could go every element of why I have the copy above the shipping box and why it talks about order speed, about why we did a button color. Like, everything I could tell you the story behind it, because there's so many different tests and variations of the people I met and things we figured out. But as a whole, if you look at this, like, this is the framework. This is what it looks like. Remember I told you earlier, like, all these funnel things are frameworks? This is the framework of a successful page for an unboxing funnel. Okay, video, two-step order form, like, the headline there, like, the structure has been tested more than probably anything that you've ever dreamt of. And this is, like, the control right now. Okay, and hopefully, somebody will beat the control, but this is the control. So this is where we start from and we try to beat it from here. Okay? So that's the first page here, the two-step order form. A couple key things, again, headline, video, all the arrows pushing my eyes towards the order form. And then there's the order form over there. Step number one, we're getting shipping address, and then step number two, we get their credit card information. Okay, so that's number one. A couple other things on this page. If you start looking at my pages closer, you'll see more fun things. I'm going to map this out so I can explain it while I write it. In every sales process we do, there are three things I try to do. Okay, number one, the most powerful way to sell anybody anything is through emotion. Okay? So emotion is the best. That's why when I talked to you guys on session number one about stories telling, I'm telling a story to try to invoke an emotion to get you to do something, right? We talk about we don't use logic to sell, we use emotion. Emotion is the core thing, right? And so emotion is why people buy. Okay, we always say people buy things emotionally and they try to justify them logically. So the best tool I have to sell the masses is emotion. So that's where I lead with, okay? Then what happens is there's a percentage of people, even if they get emotionally engaged, like this is amazing, I want it, and then all of a sudden this logical thing pops up and they're like, oh no, how do I justify this to my wife or my spouse or myself or my bank or my accountant or whatever, right? And the logical brain starts going like crazy. So the next close is the logical close. We'll put glasses on the logical guy because they're smarter, right? So next is the logical close, okay? And it's not nearly as powerful, but it is important because a bunch of people who, like, even if they're emotionally sold, the logic, they're going to like talk themselves out of it, right? And then the third over here is the emotion, sorry, fear. How do I make a guy look fearful? He's got a furrowed brow. Is he like fearful? I'm not that good of a drawer. Anyway, that guy is scared, so he looks pretty scared, right? I don't even know. There you go. That guy's scared. Okay, so we've got emotion, logic, and fear. Okay, and fear, you can put in here, you can put in urgency and scarcity. Okay? So if I'm trying to sell something to somebody, my very first pass is like, let me emotionally convince you of this thing. So I can emotionally sell you. And again, a bunch of people, that's the big domino, knocks down and they're going to go buy. And the next wave of people, or the logical ones, are like, I don't know about this. And if I can logically convince them, knocks them down, boom, and the next percentage comes. And the last is people who are like, even if they're in here or here, they're like, I'll do it later. And they're like, they can't make a decision to freak it out. And urgency and scarcity, they push them over. Okay? And if you look at percentage-wise, I would say you're looking at probably, I don't know, I'm going to guess. Let's say 40% here, 30% here, 30% here. That equals 100, right? Yeah, okay. I'd say it's probably around this, okay? 40% of your buyers come from a logical, from emotion. 30% come from logical, and last 30 come from urgency and scarcity. So what most people do is they just do one, or the other, or the other, right? And the logical is the work unless they've already gone through the emotional first. The fear doesn't work unless they've gone through these firsts as well. So this is the sequence. So if you look at any email follow-up sequence, I go emotion, my first couple of emails, then I move to logic, and I end up with fear. If you watch my stage presentation, I start with emotion, I move to logic, I move to fear. Watch how I sell coaching, emotion. Like everything happens, like this is the process, okay? Motion, logic, fear, motion, logic, fear. Okay? So understand that in all your selling, that's always happening. In the Traffic Seekers book that you guys will be getting here in the near future, I go deep into this, and I show how we use it, even in retargeting ads. Everything is you're moving from emotion to logic to fear. Your best case is selling some of the emotion, then it shifts to logic, and then it goes to fear. Okay? So what's interesting is if you look at the layout of most of our sales pages, but especially our two-step order form pages, is the top block here, the top third, this is like a video here of me and the two-step order form, and this whole focus here is all about emotion, right? So if you notice, the top one third here, which is a smile, is me on the video, like, hey, there's Russell Brunson, and I'm emotionally trying to tell you a story to get you to buy. Okay? And for the percentage of the people, the majority of people will buy right here, and then people are like, oh, that's amazing, like, ah, but what, what's the catch, what, you know, right? They have all the things to keep them, so they start scrolling, that's your scrollers. They scroll past this, and then this next thing, okay, the emotional people didn't even see this, they bought, like, they're ready to go and take over the world whatever it is you just sold them, right? The next people though are looking for logic, so here's where you have them, like, all your copy that's speaking to their logical mind, okay? In fact, I'll even say a lot of times, like, logically, you know, if you want to, logically you know that funnels are a great way to grow your company, logically if you really want to do this, you've got to work with a company who, who's helped more people hit million or stash the funnels than anybody else on earth, right? Like, it just makes logical sense, like, I'll even use the word logical because that's who I'm speaking to, I'm speaking to the logical mind, okay? So I'm trying to get them to be able to justify this to themselves, to their spouse, to their account, to whoever it is that they've got to justify things logically too, okay? In fact, I heard someone tell me this one time about this, like, buying cars, right? Like, you go into the car dealership and you see the new car, it could be whatever it is for you, it could be a Ferrari, a McLaren, a Lamborghini, maybe it's a minivan, like, everyone's got their thing, right? You see the car and you're like, oh, this would be so amazing, you're pitching yourself, driving the car and like, the wind blowing through your hair and all the amazing stuff, right? And you're emotionally so sold on it, and you go and you buy it, you drive it home and your spouse is like, did you just buy that? You're like, oh yeah, but you have to understand, like, it gets really good gas mileage, like, it's the best, most safest cars in the world, and like, you logically try to convince them of what the emotional decision you just made, right? Because we buy things emotionally and we justify them logically, okay? So that's what's happening is I'm giving them the ability to justify it to themselves or the person they have to justify this purchase to later, right? And then the third thing is all about fear, right? So this is where my urgency and scarcity comes. This is where it's like, like, why are you gonna buy right now and they call the action and how many are left, like, all the things that cause urgency and scarcity and fear, and that's the third thing, okay? So if you look at the funnel box, that's the layout of a page, emotion, logic, and then the fear, okay? All right, so that's page number one. You guys didn't know there was all this stuff going into, huh? You're like, oh, we're also through a funnel page. It's like, no, you have to understand the frameworks, right? Here's the frameworks. It was happening every single page, and how it all weaves in. Now it's like, you gotta take your art, like, what is your emotional story that you're gonna plug in here? What's your logical arguments on why in the world they should buy this thing? Okay, what's urgency, scarcity, you're gonna create. This is where you weave your art into this framework, but understand this is the framework, right? It's a page, it's a process, things like that. Now you gotta weave your stories, your art, your stuff into that framework, right? You're gonna paint the bathroom whatever color you want it to be in this house, but the framework's there, okay? And so I showed you some of this homepage, but this is a theme that you're gonna see throughout. It's in every email sequence, it's in every page, upsell page, it's downsell page, like throughout everything we're doing, you're gonna follow this emotional logic fear path, okay? Over and over and over again. Okay, all right, let me move to the next slide. So there's the two step order form we talked about here. Okay, again, step one, they put in the shipping address, then it automatically directs from step two where they put in their credit card information. Okay, now, I wanna show you this one thing we figured out. I can't even remember exactly where we figured this whole thing out. I'm pretty dang sure that I gave it the name order form bump. In fact, I would bet because I had some friends who, they did a lot of infomercials back in the day. And so what they would do is, if you ever call an infomercial, they call you on the phone, you're like, hey, I wanna order this book or this cooker or this juice machine, whatever you call it on the phone. And then the sales scripts, the way they're written on the phone is they create this thing, they call them bumps. So the bump is like, oh, do you want rush shipping? Oh, cool, hey, do you want the cookbook to go along as well? Oh, cool, and they call them bumps instead of upsells. And so when we started building this, because I had friends who always called them bumps on the phone, I was like, let's put a bump into our funnel here. Now, the reason why order form bumps are so powerful is back when I was a young in this industry, and we were first learning about this whole funnel concept. This is what happened, is somebody had a page where they sold something and they made money. They're like, this is amazing. And then somebody had an idea, like let's put an upsell. So they made upsell, OTO, one time offer, and they sold something more. Oops, there's a person, and they sold more, and they're like, oh my gosh, like one third of the people who bought this also bought this. And I made more money, just kidding, more money. They made more money. And like this is amazing, like what if we did, instead of one upsell, what if we did two upsells? And like, oh, that's the greatest, they did two upsells, right? OTO two. And also like one fifth of people bought this, and they made what? More money, like this is amazing. We had two upsells, what if we had a downsell here? And what if we had a downsell here? What if we had another upsell? And what if we had another upsell? And all of a sudden what was created were these funnels. I had a friend who started videotaping funnels, and there are people who go through like a three to four hour long process to get through all the upsells. They just call this upsell, anyone guess what the next word is? Upsell held. Okay, and I got caught in the trap, I was like, well, what's everyone's doing? So I got to have more upsells than the last guy. So I used to have every single funnel I'd have, I would have upsell, downsell, upsell, downsell, upsell, downsell. So if someone went through everything, said no to everything, I had six offers. And I was like, oh, that plus the first thing they just bought, right? And I was like, this is amazing. Like we're making so much money. And then one day I had these friends, this is like a decade ago, I had these friends, they created this new software that would do rates. It's actually shopapproved.com. It's really cool software. And they said, hey Russell, this is a company about to launch, we want you to try it out. They said, go and take our little shop approved widget and put it on your thank you page. So the last page here, put it on your thank you page. And basically people can rate you based on their experience. I'm like, oh, cool, like we'll get all of our raving customers who just bought to tell us how great we are, and they can use that widget and plug it in somewhere. And so I put on the thing during the middle of our launches, people bought the product with their six upsell, downsell things. They lined up on the thank you page and they were like, hey, what was your experience like today? Rate it. And I had no idea how upset my customers were. About 24 hours into it, I logged in to read the reports. And the majority of the people who made it through upsell hell ended up here and they hated me. I was making money, our cart value is huge. I was so excited until I found out all my customers hated me. And also I realized, oh my gosh, the lifetime value, the short term, how much money I made in the short term increase. The lifetime value of a customer died. We're talking about value ladders and taking somebody on this journey. I've had people who have been in click phones for five years, almost coming on six years. People go to every single phone I can find. Why? Because they have an amazing experience. We serve the highest level possible. And I realized that all these people who went through this process, by the time they end over here, they are not happy with me and the experience was bad. And I was like, oh my gosh, I got to do something. And so I started looking at it and I said, well, this is a bad experience. What should I do? I said, okay, well, I'm going to cut this upsell and this upsell and cut the downsells and downsells I hear. And I kept testing and testing and we tested a ton this for probably two years or so. And we found, when all of a sudden done, we found that for me, and again, this isn't going to be different for everybody. Some industries are different too. Sometimes you can do more or less. But my own personal rule for my funnels is I never want to have an offer and I will max have two upsells. That's where I found that the maximum number of money for me and still happiness for my customers over here. It was like, that was the sweet spot. If I went beyond that, that's when I started getting angrier and angrier and just ruined my whole company. When I kept it to the product and two upsells max, that's when people were happy and they would continue my value ladder and everything was good. So that's a big lesson I learned. I wanted to share with you guys because sometimes you can use these funnels and you can make a lot of money, but you can destroy your relationship with your customers and you make money in the short term and not the long term. I don't know about you, but I'm in the long term game. This is not a short term transactional thing for me. Otherwise I'd be charging you this very second to be here. The reason why you get to be here and going through this is because I know that the happier you are here, the more likely you're going to build funnels. More likely you build funnels on this platform. More likely you're going to go to Funnel Flicks. More likely you're going to buy another course in the future. More likely you're going to go to Funnel Flicks. More likely you're going to buy all the other things happen, the happier experience you have. So if I take some short term money loss here, it's worth it because the long term, lifetime value of the customer dramatically goes up. Does that make sense? So that's kind of the game plan. So for me, it was always like this frustrating thing of like, I miss the days when I was getting $200 average cart value, but I like the fact that my customers are happy when I make more of my long term. But I'm always trying to figure out, is there another way? And so this is when the order form bump came into existence. So I'm telling this whole story. The order form bump is basically another upsell that nobody sees as an actual upsell. It's like the closest thing to free money you will ever get where you can increase how much money you make and keep your customers happy. And you're getting a whole third upsell that's like sneaky stealth attack. This is where the order form bump comes from. Think about when you're at the grocery store, right? You're going around in the shopping cart, you're buying all your stuff, you're feeling your thing in, you get to the very end, and you start going through checkout line, you're walking through it, and also you sit there in the line, you look to your left, and you're like, oh, there's a national inquire in the Star magazine. And people, and all of the gossip magazines right there, you look at the right hand, boom, you see Tic Tacs, and you see gum, and you see Purell hand sanitizer, and you see bottles of all the impulse buys, right? And people are like, oh, I'm going to get that. And this, and this, and this. And they grab it right there. That's the equivalent of an order form bump. Someone's coming through your funnel, and also they stop and they're like, oh, what's that? Boom, that's the order form bump. It's getting them just enough attention, they don't have to think about it. They're just looking at it like, oh, I want that as well. There's no sales letter for Tic Tacs. You're like, oh, those look really good. I'm going to get the orange ones because they taste like candy. They are candy, but I think they're mint because they've tricked me, right? So you just grab it really quick off the shelf. That's what an order form bump is. If the thing you're selling is complex and it will not work as an order form bump, it needs to be something like Tic Tacs where you're like, oh, do you also want this? Like, oh, yeah, I do. So let me show you what an order form bump looks like if you're not familiar. The order form bump is very strategic where it fits. It fits after someone's putting their credit card information. They've already put the commitment to put their credit card information in, but before the submit button. Right now, we've tested above, every variation, like that's the sweet spot right there. They come in there and they're buying the product. They put their credit card and it's like, oh, you're real quick. Do you want to upgrade your order? Boom, here's the thing. And this has got to be, again, your Tic Tac offer where it's just like, it's simple. It's a one sense to explain it. If it takes three senses, explain it too much. It's like, oh, hey, you just bought this book on whatever you want, the audio book, or you want the templates, or you want the, it's a very simple offer, right? And it gives you this extra upsell in here that doesn't, again, increases your money but does not decrease happiness level of people. This is like, for most people, most businesses, the order form bump will cover the cost of your advertising. This makes your rest of your funnel profitable. It's like the secret sauce. Anyway, so that's the order form bump. And here's a couple of other examples. I'm just like really quick, like yes, Russell, up give me order. Add this to it. Yes, Russell, up give me order. Up give me order. If you don't have an order form bump on every order form, you're literally just throwing money away. It does nothing but give you more money. It does not decrease happiness of your customers. It just gives you more money and gives them another opportunity to buy something from you. So that's the order form bump. It's like the greatest gift that we could possibly give you. Again, I'm building click, not me. I didn't build click phones. When Todd was building click phones, he was like, please give me an order form bump. Please, we got to figure this out. So he put an order form bump and now it's so simple. Click a button. It's an every single order form and that's literally free money. It covers your ad cost in most situations. So that's the key, order form bumps. All right, so after you bought your product, you bought your product, they saw the order form up, now we're taking it to our upsell process. We use the words upsell and one time offer, OTO synonymously. Same thing, special one time offer, special upsell doesn't matter. You'll hear both those terms inside the industry used all the time, one time offer, OTO or upsell. Now, with information product, and this is one of the differences between a book funnel and a cart funnel. With information products, the secret, people always ask me, if they just bought a $10 book, what price point should I sell the next product? I'm like, no, you understand, that's not the right question to be asking. It's not about the price point. The question is, what is the next logical thing that they need on their journey? So say they came right here and they saw your thing and they're like, this is awesome and they bought, right? And then order form bump, you take the offer right here. And now they're coming through, so you have to think, hey, what's the next logical thing that they need to be successful? And I don't care if it's $1,000 or $2, the next logical thing is what should be right here. The first time I got this, it's been a while since I learned this lesson, but somebody was talking, I think it might have been Mike Geary, anyway, whoever it was, thank you for telling me this concept. It's mental, I can't even remember exactly what it was, but the concept was, let's say someone comes to your funnel, right? And let's say you have an ebook, like how to get six pack abs, like sweet, I got six pack abs and you bought the product. Now what we think sometimes, like, well, someone, oh, that person's interested in six pack abs. I'm going to sell them some more six pack abs training here. This is my six pack abs home study course. What you understand is that if you put yourself in the mind of your consumer, as soon as they bought this thing, they're like, I need six pack abs. They bought it, they put their credit card, they bought it. In their mind now, they have six pack abs. This promise has been fulfilled. In their mind, like, I got six pack abs. You come here like, hey, do you want my six pack abs home study course? They're like, I already have abs. I bought them a minute ago. Like, why would I buy this thing? Like, I already bought that. Okay? Their itch has already been scratched. That's what you got to understand. Okay? The first time I got this is when we launched 108 Split Test Book. I had 108 Split Test Book and the upsell was our conversion course for 297 bucks. And almost nobody bought this. I was so confused. And so I started emailing the customers like, why did you not buy this? You told me you're interested in Split Test and said, well, I bought your Split Test Book. I already have a Split Test. I don't need to test something else. I was like, crap. So the question is like, what's the next logical thing? If I just got six pack abs, what's the next thing I need? Well, it's like, hey, you're going to go out and shop. Or you're going to go eat at fast food restaurants. How do you find the food that's actually healthy? Like, oh my gosh, that's a good point. I'm going to be at fast food. That is the next thing. I have six pack abs. I got to keep them. Well, if I'm going to eat fast food, oh my gosh, I need that. Boom. They're going to buy that product. The next logical thing. They bought the .com secrets book. I got to build funnels. If my next thing is more funnels, they're not going to buy it. They bought funnels in their mind that itch has been scratched. What's the next logical thing they need? Oh, if you have funnels, you need traffic. Or if you have funnels, you need copy. It's the next logical thing. So when you look at an info product book style unboxing funnel, it's always about what's the next thing they need. Now, for this upsell, what's the next logical thing they need? If you don't need that, what's the next thing? Anyway, I bought .com secrets of the book. And I bought the copywriting course and how to put the words on the pages. Now I need the traffic course. That's the next logical thing. So that's more important is understanding the logical sequencing of events than understanding the price points. This one could be 50 bucks. It could be 500 bucks. This could be 500 bucks. It doesn't matter about that. It just matters if it logically makes sense sequentially. Does that make sense? The other thing is that if you go back to slides over here, on the upsells, these videos are typically very simple. It's not me. In fact, in .com secrets book, you'll get the scripts for how we do the upsell videos. It's not me repitching everything. They already bought this thing. It's just saying, hey, thanks so much for buying the book you just bought. The next people I was asking after the book is like, well, how do I write the copy for the pages? So let me tell you about this thing I created for you. Boom. So it's a faster upsell. The upsell videos are typically two to three minutes long max. They're not longer than that. I'm not trying to resell on something. They already bought the first thing. I'm just trying to push them over the edge. So like, oh, you also need this as well. Like, oh, yeah, that makes sense. Oh, yeah, that makes sense. Okay. That's how you position the upsell offers. Okay. So I told you before, I always do, I do max offer a bump in two upsells. Sometimes I send in two upsells. I'll kill this one right here. And instead what I'll do is I'll add a downsell. Okay. So I kill this right here. And then this is my offer by bump in my upsell, my downsell. That'll be all that I do. Okay. So what a downsell is basically is typically it's like, they said no to this and saying, okay, you didn't want this product for $100. What if I gave you the digital version for $50? Or maybe you said no to this because you can't afford $100. How about we do three payments of $37, right? So I'm usually downselling either a digital version or a payment plan or pricing, but it's basically just a downsell like that. It's very simple, very easy. That's usually what my downsell offers are. Okay. And then from here again, upsell number two, if I had that, is the next logical thing. Okay. So that's kind of how we do these, how we do a book funnel. The one last thing that we'll do a lot of times, and this comes back to, I showed you guys, not on this page, but if I showed you guys, or this page, we've been doodling a lot today. Back here, as I've gone through this offer on the thank you page, a lot of times I'll introduce them to the next funnel. Okay. So for example, I call this the thank you page webinar. That's why I use the expert secrets book. The thank you page on my suite, thank you for buying the book. Thanks for everything. We appreciate you as a customer. Right now, I'm going to let your training start today because obviously you're excited about this. So I wait for the book to show up. We're going to start this training right here, right now. Let's go. And then I will go and I'll jump right immediately into training. And that training is typically a presentation that then sells them on my next thing. So for me, this is like an hour long or a 90-minute long webinar about expert secrets. And I talk about that and then click funnels and then at the end of it I make a special offer for click funnels. Because it's like broken and it's perceived as training, it seems like another different funnel and people don't get upset. They go through an hour of like training before I ever make an offer. So it's just the next thing. The thank you page webinar is the thing that we use to bridge them to the next stage in the value ladder. Okay. So again, the goal of that is to invite your customers to keep moving through. Okay. So I want to show you why this is so vitally important. The book funnel or unboxing funnels like this. I'm going to show you guys this in relation to a typical author. Okay. So a typical author, like here's some of my friends who have written books and they're all on Amazon. The books are selling like 15 to 20 bucks, right? So they've got $15 to spend to sell a book, right, in advertising. And so that's a problem that I'm going to show you why here in a second. But that's the biggest problem my most authors struggle at selling books. People always ask me, like, how in the world have you sold so many copies of your book? Like, because I don't rely on Amazon. I create funnels that make, give me the ability to sell unlimited number of my books. We sell thousands of copies every single week now for five years because of these book funnels. Okay. So funnels make it possible to sell a lot of books with no advertising budget. So I'm going to walk you through the math and the metrics and just hope you understand book funnels and cart funnels a little bit better. Okay. So here's the math, basically how the whole thing works. Okay. CPA stands for cost per acquisition. How much money does it cost me to get a customer? Okay. I'll break all the details. But that's the most expensive cost. ACV is my average cart value. So on average, every single person goes through this funnel. How much money do I make from them? Okay. So I'm going to show you guys how this all, this works. Okay. So number one, right now we spend about $23 on average on Facebook to sell a copy of one of my books. Okay. So that's why, that's my bounty I pay Zuckerberg. Zuckerberg, here's $23. He's like, cool, here's the book customer. Okay. So it's costing me $23. If you think about it, if you're selling your book on Amazon and you sell $15, you're already in the hole right there. Right? You pay Zuckerberg $23, you go to Amazon, he buys $15, you just lost whatever the difference is on there. It's been too long of a day for me to do the math in my head right now. But that's why most people can't scale their book campaigns or any campaign, right? So instead we start selling through a funnel. So that works. Come back here. Here's my, here's my book funnel, right? So the very first page here they come and they put in their credit card and the book is free. They just cover $7.95 shipping and handling. Okay. So far on average I know about 13% of people who land on this page will give me the $7.95 of shipping and handling. Okay. So they've given me $7.95. So my average card value so far is just $7.95. For each customer that came through I've only made $7.95. Okay. So I spent $23, I made $7.95. So right now I'm in the hole, right? It's like, crap, I'm not, I'm not going to be successful. Now this is where the funnel starts taking place. Okay. So 13% of people bought the book. From that 20.8% of people also said yes to the order form bump. See, they pay an extra $37. I said yes, so I also went back as well. Okay. So 20.8% of people bought the upsell. So now I take the $7.95 plus the 20.8% of $37 equals, if I do the math down there, now my average card value is $15.65. Okay. So I spent the $27. I made $15.60. So I'm still not a break even yet, but just wait, we're just in the very beginning of the funnel. Okay. So now we go to the next step after the order form bump. Now I go to upsell number one. It's a $97 course. I'm right on 9.92% of my customers by the upsell in $97 bucks. Okay. So I take that times $97 times 992. It means I made an additional $9.62, which basically means my average card value now is $25.27. So I spent $27. I made $25. So I'm still in the hole. And this is where upsell number two comes and saves the day. Upsell number two happens. It's a $297 course. 4.19% of people take the second upsell. Now my average card value bumps up to $37.71. So if you look at that, it means I spent $23 to Zuckerberg on Facebook. I made $37.71 on average for every single book that I sold, which means I made $14.71 and I got a customer. Okay. So you see how that works? When I do it that way, I don't have an advertising budget. Because they say, well, how much money do you spend on every book, every day on books? I'm like, well, if every time I spend $23 and make $37.71, I make $14 for every book I sell, I can sell as many as I want. I'm going to sell 1,000 a week, 2,000 a week, 5,000 a week. Okay. I just keep cranking up the ad spend as much as I can possibly handle. That is the big secret. So if you look at this, when your average card value is bigger than your ad spend, you can grow your list without any limits. Does that make sense? So that's the magic you have to start understanding that. When I spend $27 to get a customer and I make $37, I get cash and I get a customer and now I can start sending off the value ladder. From the last session, we talked about the value of the list. A list is valuable, it's even more valuable than a list is the buyer list. These are the people who have paid money for information. They're more valuable to me. Okay. And so that's what these book funnels do is they create customers for you. Okay. All right. So that is the book funnel. You guys see how that works? Now, lots of people talk about the book funnel is going to be almost identical in the cart funnel. So I'm going to show you some examples here and I'll talk about the differences. The cart funnel is for e-commerce people. For most people who are selling right now on Shopify, on Amazon, on Etsy, things like that, for the most part, this is the funnel you're going to be using. It works. It's magic. I can show you so many of our two column cup winners who just plugged in their products in these cart funnels and they just work. Okay. We got to understand the psychology behind this. Let me walk you through how it works. Okay. So here's the cart funnel. Once again, it has more than three pages but that's what we got on the slides. So notice it looks almost identical to the book funnel. Okay. The structure, the framework is the same. Okay. But the art that we weave in is a little bit different. So we got to understand. Okay. The biggest difference is that, remember we talked about over here, I think it's this page right here. In an information business, they get their itch scratch and then you're going, what's the next logical thing? Next logical thing. Everything is the next logical thing. With cart funnels, you're not thinking about, you're not thinking immediately about the next best thing. You're thinking about how do I give these people more at a discount. Okay. So that's the big thing. I'll show you what I'm talking about here in a second. Okay. How do I give them more at a discount? So right here, for example, if someone comes here and they buy a flashlight or they buy a supplement. Say they bought one of my, back in the day I had a supplement company selling supplements for people with diabetic neuropathy. So I'd sell a bottle of pills right here and the upsell is like, hey, you just bought a bottle for 65 bucks a bottle. Would you like to have, to buy six more bottles at a 30% discount? Okay. Like that's what I'm talking about. It's more of the same thing at a better deal. Okay. More at a discount. So just like I showed you guys before, like Amazon book sells versus the funnel. Same thing here. This is a company we had a chance to work with called Fiber Fix. And when they sell Fiber Fix on Amazon, they're able to spend $12.99 to sell Fiber Fix. We took all the stuff you've learned about in the last couple of days about how to build an offer, how to put things together, how to bundle things together, how to make a funnel. Like we built this cart funnel and it gave people the ability to make way more money and they were able to grow and scale this campaign. So I'm going to walk you through here in a second. Okay. The question then is if I'm doing a cart funnel, like what is the thing that goes first? Okay. I always talk to e-commerce people. Like I've got a thousand different skews. I've got 12 skews or all these things. And like, you know, I have e-commerce store. I have a Shopify store and Amazon store because I send everybody here and they pick what they want. Okay. If I am working on a cart funnel, the first thing I do is I figure out what is the number one selling product that I have? What's the sexiest thing? The best thing? People that most of my customers want. Okay. So you got to figure out. We did that. We had a chance for Marcus Lamonus to go do a funnel for Camping World. And so I talked to their chief staff on the phone ahead of time that ran their web store and they literally had like 10,000 skews, right? For Camping World. Like everything you can dream of. And I asked the guy, I'm like, what's your best selling? He's like this and they're like all these different things. Some things were big RVs. Some things were septic tank pumps and just like insane all the different things that they had. I was like, oh my gosh. There's like that's a ton of different stuff. And so then I said, well, of everything in your entire store, what's the number one selling product? And he kind of laughed. He's like, honestly, I'm like, yeah. And he's like, it's this dissolving toilet paper. I was like, are you serious? Like, yeah, we sell more of that than anything else in the store, like volume wise. That's amazing. So then I was like, I need to do some more market research. So we actually went down to Camping World. We walked in this huge store looking at all this stuff, walking around and the first employee comes up to me like, hey, can I help you? I'm like, you guys have a really random question of everything in the store and all the shelves. What's the number one selling thing you have here? And they came back and said, well, the number one selling thing of everything we have is our dissolving toilet paper. I was like, are you serious? This is awesome. So I was walking around the store some more and another employee came up. He said, I can help you. I'm like, I got a question for you. I asked the same question. They said, yeah, dissolving toilet paper. Every single person we talked to said dissolving toilet paper is the best product on all the thing. They said, cool. If I'm going to be generating people into a cart funnel, I'm taking the best selling product. So we made this funnel for them and we got the offer with four rolls of our fastest dissolving toilet paper for free. And this is the funnel that we use for Camping World. Okay? So that's the first thing. Look at all the skews we're already selling and figure out what's the best thing you have and you're going to turn that into an offer. So I'm not just going to say, here's a roll of fiber fix. I said, how do I make this a sexy offer? Because I can sell fiber fix. So I bundled it. I said, how do I do this? So we looked for this offer. We said, okay, well, what are all the skews they have? And they said, okay, we've got two-inch wrap, four-inch wrap, all these things. So I said, okay, how about this? I said, take three of your one-inch fiber fix today. That was their sexiest product still the most. You buy three of them today. We're going to give you two wraps of two, throw two wraps of two-inch for free and one wrap of four-inch for free. Okay? So they're going to get five of those things for the price of three. So now it becomes an offer. It's sexy. It's exciting. You can't buy this now. You can't buy it at your own store. This is the only place to get this offer. Right? No longer is it a product. Now it's not a commodity anymore. Now it's an offer. Okay? So it made an offer. That was the first step here. Okay? Now the next thing is we do the two-step order form. Now a couple of things you can do in here. The two-step order form at the top of here, if you look at here, you can have people choose different products. So we said, okay, well, you can buy three fiberfix wrapping and get three for free or you can buy six and get six for free. We can buy nine to get nine for free or 12, 15, or 30. So we gave them whole bunch of options to buy more of the same thing at a discount. Okay? And by default, the majority of people took that middle route where they bought nine. So now they're spending $74.97 on average as opposed to they went to Amazon. The best case scenario in Amazon is you spend $12.97. We're here, the majority of our people now spend $74 on fiberfix before they even get into the cart. Right? Right out of the gate. So we have the product selector up front where we bundled more at a discount. And then down below we have the order form bump. It was like, hey, do you also want to throw in a heat wrap? Okay? This is their second best selling skew they had. It's like, hey, this is a special offer for an extra $10 we'll throw in a heat wrap. Do you want that too? And boom, a huge percent, I think it was like 30, something percent of people said yes to that as well. So right out of the gate, the majority of people are spending almost $80 before they get into the upsells versus if you go to Amazon, the best case scenario is spending $12. Okay? Do you see how this is all working? So that's happening in page one. Now we go to the upsell. And the upsell guess what we sell? We don't sell the next best thing yet because we were at e-commerce. What we did is we sold more of the same thing at a discount. So the page, if you look at it, it says, wait, grab one more starter kit for $20. They just bought I think for $27 on the page earlier. Buy one more for $20 and you get a huge discount. Plus you can get them, you can get away for gifts, for family members. You'll put these things in your car, in your house and like, it's more at a discount, right? And then you know, at the top of that there's like one more starter kit or two. We gave them two more options. Okay? And a huge set of people now, I think it was like 19% of people bought another kit right there. Okay? So average car value dramatically increased again. Okay? Then the next page, we said, okay, well now, now we know like what's the next best skew you have? What's the next best thing that people buy? And they said, well, the next best selling skew we have is this. They said, cool, do they want to buy one or two? Okay? And that became upsell number two, is they want one or two. Okay? After they finished that, I came back and said, okay, what's the next best skew they have there? This is the next best thing. So we had that as well. And then the next product, we had the order for it and they had three upsells and for their product, they were okay with that. Okay? And so that was what it worked. And then on the thank you page, we took what we call an offer wall. It says, thank you so much for your order. By the way, here's other things we have and had skews to all the other products that they sold. Okay? And that's an offer wall. It's basically a wall of like, thanks so much for your order. Here's other stuff we sell. Here's links to all the other funnels we have. You might want to go in and buy other stuff as well. Okay? And a huge set of people buy stuff on the offer wall as well. Okay? So that's kind of how it works. Okay? And here's a couple of examples. This is Jamie Cross who spoke last year's Funnel I Can Live. This is her funnel, right? On her funnel, she sold body lotion bars up front. Okay? And I can't remember exact prices, like 70-something bucks for these lotion bars. So she did the five-minute perfect webinar script. On her video, those of you guys who are at Funnel I Can Live, you remember that. That's her five-minute perfect webinar. Sells the body bar. Then she, so that's what the video or the page looks like. Okay? Notice all the same things we talked about. Video of her doing the motion of the cell, logical step down below. We've got the right-hand form over here with the step one credit card. So you step one shipping information and step two is credit card. Okay? It goes now to step number two. Which for her, she's got a product selector. So you can buy one body bar. You can buy three body bars, four body bars, or six body bars. So you have a product selector right there. The majority of people take a second option. So it doubles the order right out of the gate. And down below, she's got an order form bump as well for rush shipping. Boom. Rush shipping for an extra four bucks. Boom. Increases cart value as well. And from there, so you get up still. Hey, you just bought this stuff. You want to buy some more at a discount. Boom. Buy one or two bottles at a discount. You were paying 30 bucks or whatever. Now it's like 25 bucks at a discount because you're already a customer. So yes or no. And it takes the next page, which is like, here's the next best you have, which is this product right here. It's our face lotion bar. You want to buy this product, either one or two of them as well. People said yes. And boom, that funnel got her into the two comic club in record time. Okay? You see how this works? It works in any kind of e-commerce business as well. This is one of my favorite companies. I've tried like three times to buy it from these guys. Maybe you're watching. They're ClickFunnels members as well. But it's called Soxie. They've got the coolest socks ever. If you're looking at socks I'm wearing, they're usually always Soxie socks. But Soxie.com is the funnel as well. So you come here and you're like, hey, do you want to buy these awesome socks for six bucks? You're like, yeah, I do. So you buy the socks. It's like, hey, do you want to upsell and buy some more socks? Actually, yes, I would buy more socks. It's like, oh, here's an episode. We want another pack of six mystery socks. Like, yeah, that'd be amazing. And then they keep paying you to sell you more socks. The nice thing about socks is that people have two feet. And every day they have to put new socks on it. That's why you keep selling more of the same thing. That's the big fundamental difference between information products and physical products. It's information products. People are going to buy one thing after they're itched a scratch. They're not going to buy another thing. But physical products is different. People have different pairs of socks. They might need to fiber fix different places. Maybe we want to set it as gifts or Christmas gift. There's so many different variations. So I understand that logic of like, it's more of the same. And then what's the next skewed? The next best selling skewed. The next best selling skewed. That's how you do a cart funnel. Does that make sense? OK, so those are the two core types of unboxing funnels. We've got our book funnels. We've got our cart funnels. Very similar in nature. Structure similar, but the arts a little different. How you communicate, what you sell, what order, all those kind of things is a little bit different. The third type of funnel I want to share with you guys today is a little bit different. But it's one of my favorite. And I know you guys know that this is my favorite because we've been doing this now for like a year and a half. And it's a challenge funnel. And a challenge funnel is powerful because I think for a lot of us, it's interesting. Excuse me. With ClickFunnels, we drive leads from so many different places. We get over 1,000 people a day who sign up for ClickFunnels accounts. And so they're coming, it's just closer to 2,000 there now. They're coming from everywhere, right? We have so many funnels out there grabbing people and bringing them in. We found this is tough because some people come in one direction and they come in and they don't understand anything about funnels or marketing. They come in, they create ClickFunnels account because they think it's exciting. And then they're there for a day or two and they're like, I don't know, this doesn't make any sense. What's Russell talking about? Why is he so excited? And then they just leave. Other people come in and they go through this training and this training, this training, they show up, they get a ClickFunnels account and they understand that they got the context. They understand our language and all those things. And because of that, they stay for five years and they make millions of dollars, right? And so I was like, how do we get everybody indoctrinated so they understand our language so that they become our dream customers? How do we take all the cold hard masses and bring them to a spot where we can take them through a step-by-step process, helps develop them into our dream customers on the backside of it now, we can actually serve them. We can take them to the next level. And so I had a couple of friends doing this. The first was Natasha Hazel. She was the first one who I ever saw kind of really pioneered this. And she spoke at Laszlo's Funnel Hacking Live showing the whole model. It was amazing. And she had one of our clients, Christy Kodred, who spoke Funnel Hacking Live showing their challenge funnels as well. Garrett White launched from about the same time and I funnel hacked his like 2,500 times. How much other things and got really good at these challenge funnels. And so last year we did a test with ClickFunnels. We said, okay, let's do our first challenge funnel. We launched it. We got a ton of you guys in here. Hopefully, have you guys gone through the challenge funnel? Okay, if you've done the one funnel way challenge, now in the comments below, tell me. Like, yes, it was amazing. Yes, I did it. Whatever it is, tell us about it. But we did the first challenge. We tested it. It was last year about this time. Took a huge group of people through, did the challenge. And then we kind of waited. We waited for four or five months for it to begin. And we just started watching the stats. And I looked at how much was the person who went through the challenge funnel worth to us long term, right, through the rest of the value ladder. And by taking people through this challenge that helped take the coal hard masses, helped them to understand our language, our vocabulary, the core things like how to build an offer. What's hook story, like hook story offer. How do you build an offer? How do you create a product? Like how do you drive traffic? How do you create videos? Like how do you tell a story? All the core things that they need to be successful. We taught all those things in the chronological order. And the end of it, the end. And also now they're running on the ClickFunnels journey. A couple of things. Number one is that their success rate on the backside was so much higher than anything we did prior to that. Number two is it became more compliant customers. They understood us better. They understood how to use the software. They didn't ask, I mean, right now the biggest problem with ClickFunnels customer support is people who are asking questions about software because they understand the core fundamentals of a funnel, right? How do I do this? That's not a funnel. That'd be an e-commerce. This is how you have to strategically think about things. So customer support dropped down dramatically because they understood the core fundamentals of funnel building. Number three is that some people don't like me. They don't like my style. I talk too fast or I mumble too much or maybe they, whatever. And the people who just weren't good fits for us or our culture or our community, they left and they went away. They went through three days and they're like, this guy is annoying and they took off. And then our raving fans were developed and created and they became better customers. We looked at the lifetime value of those people over the next four, five, six months. I don't have the numbers in front of me, but dramatically, insanely higher. And then so we got so excited, we started doing it like once a month, we started doing a challenge, or once every six weeks, we do a challenge. And the same thing happened. Then every month we started doing it. And right now we have every other week a new challenge as launch. We have whole teams now doing the challenges, but it's one of the most powerful things we've added to our company at ClickFunnels. And it's just something that I think all of you guys should be looking at. Like how do you, how can you incorporate a challenge into what you're doing? Especially at the beginning tiers of your value ladder, right? Like look at, look at my value ladder. Someone comes into this. If you notice like I showed you guys the, the summit funnels, right? The summit funnels, what's the ep cell? Our challenge. Okay, our book, some of us, our book, what's the ep cell? The challenge. Like everything we're pushing people to is through a challenge. If they buy a book funnel here, I take a book funnel and then I take them through the challenge funnel. Because if I get someone to go through the challenge funnel, they're likely to success and everything else in the value ladder dramatically goes up. Okay? And so I think everybody, if you can figure out like how can you do a challenge? I see people now doing challenges for e-commerce products as well. Like how do you use our product line to be able to get this result? Okay? So the best way to do a challenge is like is, here's a good result, right? So for ours is like how to build your first or your next funnel in the next 30 days. Okay? I'll show some of those ones. Like Christy Code Red, how to lose your, your, your first or your next 10 pounds. Like something very tangible that people can look at and get excited about. Okay? We decided to call it some Comic Club reward. The World of Funnels Beneath the gaze of the Nashville Sun, a life at Funnel Hacking Live begins to take form. A sight not to be missed. Here we can see the migration of nearly 5,000 funnel hackers descend upon an unsuspecting hotel. For a brief period of four days, this habitat will dramatically change. The Funneless Hacker Form is a rare species. Whatever growing by the thousands each passing day. Throughout the year, most of us do not see them, often questioning their existence if it weren't for their countless retargeting acts. They wake in the early morning hours and are usually found in a crowded gym, mentoring the curious young hacklings, rejoicing in the sun by the watering hole for a brief moment, and of course, recording more ads because the backdrop is just stunning. They congregate in what's known to scientists as a joint venture. The outfit of choice being matching t-shirts and undergarments, signifying the bond that ties this dispersed breed together. This is amplified by a greeting ritual of an endless myriad of high fives, hugs, acro-yoga, and handshakes, from the field to the bush. In the early hours, they descend upon one single enclosure. As the doors swing open, the hackers spring into motion, flocking 30 miles per hour in a race towards seat supremacy. Only the most spirited will lay claim to a coveted view. From there, they revel at the arrival of the alphas who impart their wisdom from prior hunts. During this time, their feeding ritual is complex. Many subspecies survive on a diet of coffee and water, which often unintentionally creates a conflict of having to choose between absorbing knowledge and going to the bathroom. As the voyage comes to an end, they celebrate with a multitude of unpolished dance rituals, and whatever this is, a pleasing sensation. They have replenished their minds and spirits and are fit to return to the wild. FHL is a unique anthropological phenomenon, fit for the most peculiar of entrepreneurs.
